title: Arts officers, producers and directors
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. (Based on 37 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Compose songs, create vocal arrangements, and write music for commercial mediums, including advertising jingles and film soundtracks.
Practice performances, both individually and in rehearsal with other musicians, to master individual pieces of music and improve skills.
Transpose and rewrite music from one voice or instrument to another, modifying original musical scores in different styles to achieve desired effects.
Arrange and edit music composed by others, accommodating the specific needs of musicians and achieving desired effects.
Assign, review, and oversee staff work in areas such as scoring, arranging, copying music, and vocal coaching.
Study films or scripts to determine the use of musical scores for desired effects or moods.
Transcribe musical compositions into notation and adapt them to particular groups or styles.
Create original musical forms or write within defined musical structures (such as sonatas, symphonies, or operas).
Collaborate with colleagues, such as copyists, and participate in the completion of final scores.
Copy parts from scores for individual performers and prepare music for presentations.
Produce recordings of music and contribute to the creation of music in various settings.
Stay updated with the latest trends in music and music technology to inform artistic direction.
Perform before live audiences in various settings, demonstrating skills as soloists or group members and varying styles of presentation.
Interpret and modify music while applying knowledge of harmony, melody, and rhythm to maintain audience interest.
Explore and develop musical ideas from various sources including imagination and environmental sounds.
Fill out orchestral sketches and discuss preparations with soloists and concertmasters for performances.
Determine essential elements like voices, instruments, and tones required for a musical composition to achieve desired effects.
Use gestures to communicate aspects of the music being played, such as tempo and phrasing, during live performances or rehearsals.
Direct groups in rehearsals and performances, focusing on achieving balanced dynamics, rhythm, and tempo.
Study musical scores in detail to develop interpretations and apply elements of music theory in creating compositions.
Select music for performance, considering ensemble size and variety, and plan rehearsals and performance logistics.
Experiment with different sounds and music types using technology and instruments to evaluate new ideas.
Confer with producers and directors to define music placement in film or television and audition performers for presentations.
Conduct music lessons for specific instruments and teach musical concepts and skills for performances.
Coordinate tours and arrange logistics for concert dates, accommodations, and transportation.
Rehearse and perform administrative tasks, including applying for grants, developing budgets, and negotiating contracts related to projects.
Participate in media engagements to promote music and arrange details for performances or recordings.
Seek and learn new music suitable for live performances or recordings to enhance artistic output.
Record, mix, and edit audio material, adjusting volume and sound quality during production sessions.
Regulate volume levels and sound quality during sound recordings and performances, ensuring optimal audio output.
Diagnose and resolve media system problems and maintain production equipment in good working condition.
Coordinate the activities of writers, directors, managers, and other personnel throughout the production process, and study scripts to become familiar with production concepts and requirements.

Review performer information, such as photos, resumes, voice tapes, videos, and union membership, to decide whom to audition for parts, and audition and interview performers to match their attributes to specific roles or to increase the pool of available acting talent.

Confer with technical directors, managers, crew members, and writers to discuss details of production, such as photography, script, music, sets, and costumes, and consult with writers, producers, or actors about script changes or workshop scripts, through rehearsal with writers and actors to create final drafts.
Supervise and coordinate the work of camera, lighting, design, and sound crew members, and lead and guide the work of technical staff involved in production.
Research current trends and new technology related to production, and analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis.

Select performers for roles or submit lists of suitable performers to producers or directors for final selection, and review scripts and confer with producers to determine the types and numbers of performers required for a given production.

Prepare actors for auditions by providing scripts and information about roles and casting requirements, and contact agents and actors to provide notification of audition and performance opportunities and to set up audition times.
Negotiate contract agreements with performers, agents, or between performers and agents or production companies, and obtain rights to scripts or other required materials.

Plan details such as framing, composition, camera movement, sound, and actor movement for each shot or scene, and set up and operate equipment to produce programs or broadcast live programs from remote locations as needed.
Compile scripts, program notes, and other material related to productions, and maintain talent files that include information such as performers' specialties, past performances, and availability.
Conduct meetings with staff to discuss production progress and to ensure production objectives are attained, and evaluate new and existing programming to assess suitability and the need for changes.
Develop promotions for current programs and specials, and prepare copy and edit tape so that material is ready for broadcasting.

Review information about programs and schedules to ensure accuracy and provide such information to local media outlets, and monitor broadcasts to ensure that programs conform to station or network policies and regulations.
Direct and coordinate activities of personnel engaged in broadcast news, sports, or programming, and operate and maintain on-air and production audio equipment.

Supervise and assign duties to workers engaged in technical control and production of radio and television programs, and manage own accounts and projects, working within budget and scheduling requirements.
Coordinate activities between departments, such as news and programming, and serve as liaisons between directors, actors, and agents throughout the production process.
Create worksheets for dressing lists, show notes, or costume checks, and design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for construction, major repairs, or alterations as necessary.

Monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, providing keys or access to assigned directors, costume designers, or wardrobe mistresses/masters, and purchase, rent, or requisition costumes or other wardrobe necessities.

Provide dressing assistance to cast members or assign cast dressers to assist specific cast members with costume changes, and clean and press costumes before and after performances and perform any minor repairs.

Select and combine the most effective shots of each scene to form a logical and smoothly running story, and edit films and videotapes to insert music, dialogue, and sound effects, to arrange films into sequences, and to correct errors using editing equipment.
Study and research scripts to determine how they should be directed, and direct live broadcasts, films, and recordings, or non-broadcast programming for public entertainment or education.

Cut shot sequences to different angles at specific points in scenes, making each individual cut as fluid and seamless as possible, and trim film segments to specified lengths and reassemble segments in sequences that present stories with maximum effect.

Collaborate with production designers, costume designers, or other production staff to discuss and execute costume design details, and check the appearance of costumes on stage or under lights to determine whether desired effects are being achieved.

Monitor and review programming to ensure that schedules are met, guidelines are adhered to, and performances are of adequate quality, and check completed program logs for accuracy and conformance with Federal Communications Commission (FCC) rules and regulations.

Negotiate with parties, including independent producers and the distributors and broadcasters who will be handling completed productions, and write and submit proposals to bid on contracts for projects.
Write and edit news stories from information collected by reporters and other sources, and teach acting classes.

Review film daily to check on work in progress and to plan for future filming, and develop marketing plans for finished products, collaborating with sales associates to supervise product distribution.
Arrange financing for productions, and participate in the hiring, training, scheduling, or supervision of alteration workers.
Discuss the sound requirements of pictures with sound effects editors, and set up and execute video transitions and special effects using computers to manipulate pictures as necessary.

Conduct film screenings for directors and members of production staffs, and identify and approve equipment and elements required for productions, such as scenery, lights, props, costumes, choreography, and music.
Create custom illustrations or other graphic elements, and work with creative directors to develop design solutions.
Review illustrative material to determine if it conforms to standards and specifications, and present final layouts to clients for approval.
Attend photo shoots and printing sessions to ensure that the products needed are obtained, and review and approve art materials, copy materials, and proofs of printed copy developed by staff members.
Test equipment to ensure proper operation, and monitor network transmissions for advisories concerning daily program schedules, program content, special feeds, or program changes.
Develop ideas for programs and features that a station could produce, and evaluate new and existing programming to assess suitability and the need for changes.

Hold auditions for parts or negotiate contracts with actors determined suitable for specific roles, and hire principal cast members and crew members, such as art directors, cinematographers, and costume designers.
Hire and supervise workers who help locate people with specified attributes and talents, and arrange for or design screen tests or auditions for prospective performers.

Direct setup of remote facilities and install or cancel programs at remote stations, and discuss filter options, lens choices, and the visual effects of objects being filmed with photography directors and video operators.

Select plays, scripts, books, news content, or ideas to be produced, and review scripts or other production information to determine a story's locale or period, as well as the number of characters and required costumes.
Create and maintain logs, records, or databases of information related to services rendered, payments received, and advertising efforts.
Develop written contracts for bookings and manage communication with clients or venue owners to determine event details such as preferences, scheduling, and attendance.
Collect historical cost data to estimate costs for current or future products and prepare estimates used for management planning, including labor and materials.
Schedule promotional or performance engagements for clients and arrange meetings concerning issues involving their clients.
Negotiate with managers, promoters, and other parties regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations to ensure favorable conditions for clients.
Analyze pricing or risks of services offered and assess cost-effectiveness of products, tracking actual costs relative to bids.
Gather and assess structural or location details for performances to ensure they meet production requirements.
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of industry trends and developments in order to advise clients effectively and devise strategies for their careers.
Conduct auditions or interviews to evaluate potential clients as well as follow up with promotional materials or sales strategies.
Prepare promotional plans, sales literature, and media kits while discussing buy-in options with clients for advertisements and their effectiveness.
Confer with clients to develop strategies for their careers and to explain actions taken on their behalf in relation to marketing and promotions.

Communicate with key stakeholders to determine project requirements and objectives; negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials; assign duties or responsibilities to project personnel; prepare and submit budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports; report project status to managers; and monitor project milestones and deliverables.

Plan, schedule, or coordinate project activities to meet deadlines; monitor the performance of project team members to provide performance feedback; and propose, review, or approve modifications to project plans.

Submit project deliverables to clients, ensuring adherence to quality standards; produce and distribute project documents; and create project status presentations for delivery to customers or project personnel.

Recruit or hire project personnel; identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules; and identify, review, or select vendors or consultants to meet project needs.
Schedule or facilitate project meetings; confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems; and monitor costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues.

Analyze new legislation to determine impact on risk exposure; consult financial literature to ensure use of the latest models or statistical techniques; develop contingency plans to deal with emergencies; and perform risk analyses so that appropriate countermeasures can be developed.

Investigate products or markets to determine areas for opportunity or viability for merchandising specific products, using online or offline sources; and promote products in online communities through weblog or discussion-forum postings, e-mail marketing programs, or online advertising.
Develop reports or presentations to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives; and create or maintain plans or other documents related to sustainability projects.
Participate in online forums or conferences to stay abreast of online retailing trends, techniques, or security threats.
Receive and process payments from customers, using electronic transaction services; and disclose merchant information and terms and policies of transactions in online or offline materials.
Examine merchandise to ensure correct pricing and display, and that it functions as advertised; and formulate pricing policies on merchandise according to profitability requirements.

Create marketing or outreach media, such as brochures or websites, to communicate sustainability issues, procedures, or objectives; and collect information about waste stream management or green building practices to inform decision makers.

Investigate sources, such as auctions, estate sales, liquidators, wholesalers, or trade shows for new items, used items, or collectibles; and create or distribute offline promotional material, such as brochures, pamphlets, business cards, stationary, or signage.

Attend professional meetings, read literature, and participate in training or other educational offerings to keep abreast of new developments and technologies related to disaster recovery and business continuity; and create or administer training and awareness presentations or materials.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel; and provide staff with assistance in performing difficult or complicated duties.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales, inventory-taking, reconciling cash receipts, or performing specific services.
Conduct or oversee contingency plan integration and operation; and review existing disaster recovery, crisis management, or business continuity plans.
Review and revise sustainability proposals or policies; and research or review regulatory, technical, or market issues related to sustainability.

Conduct security audits to identify potential vulnerabilities related to physical security or staff safety; monitor the work of contractors in the design, construction, and startup phases of security systems; and develop or review specifications for design or construction of security systems.
Prepare documentation for case reports or court proceedings; and maintain and update organization information technology applications and network systems blueprints.

Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends, patterns, or warnings indicating threats to security of people, assets, information, or infrastructure; and train personnel in security procedures or use of security equipment.

Develop emergency management plans for recovery decision making and communications, continuity of critical departmental processes, or temporary shut-down of non-critical departments to ensure continuity of operation and governance; and design or implement products and services to mitigate risk or facilitate use of technology-based tools and methods.
Write grant applications, rebate applications, or project proposals to secure funding for sustainability projects.
Analyze details of sales territories to assess their growth potential and to set quotas; and monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met.
Plan and prepare work schedules, and assign employees to specific duties; and attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments.

Analyze impact on, and risk to, essential business functions or information systems to identify acceptable recovery time periods and resource requirements; and develop disaster recovery plans for physical locations with critical assets, such as data centers.

Prepare or organize online storefront marketing material, including product descriptions or subject lines, optimizing content to search engine criteria; and keep records pertaining to purchases, sales, and requisitions.

Formulate pricing policies on merchandise according to profitability requirements; examine merchandise to ensure correct pricing and display, and that it functions as advertised; and analyze the performance of project team members to ensure effectiveness.
Canceled orders based on customer requests or inventory or delivery problems.
